# Citation and Attribution {.unnumbered}

## Using This Framework

The OpenData Package Developer's Guide was created to make it easier for others to build high-quality OpenData packages for Socrata-powered portals.

You are encouraged to adapt and extend the framework for your own city, organization, or educational setting. The goal of this project is to grow a consistent ecosystem of OpenData packages that share a common design philosophy while serving different communities.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## If You Build a Package

If your package is based on this framework, consider acknowledging the guide in one of the following places:

- your package `README`

- your package website

- the package vignette

- academic publications describing the package

For example:

> This package was developed using *The OpenData Package Developer's Guide* by Christian Martinez.

or

> This package was built using the OpenData package framework introduced by Christian Martinez.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Citation

If this guide contributed substantially to your package or research, please cite:

@Martinez2026OpenDataGuide

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The OpenData Ecosystem

Every package built using this framework contributes to a growing ecosystem of tools that make municipal Open Data more accessible.

Rather than maintaining dozens of unrelated implementations, this framework encourages consistency across packages while allowing each city to highlight its own data resources.

If you develop a new package using this guide, consider letting the project know. We would love to include it in the growing list of OpenData packages.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Related Resources

Additional resources related to this guide include:

- **Reproducible Research Using R** @Martinez2026ReproducibleResearch — A companion textbook covering Quarto, reproducible workflows, and scientific communication.

- **OpenData Packages** @R-nycOpenData; @R-nysOpenData; @R-mtaOpenData; @R-chiOpenData; @R-laOpenData — Reference implementations of the architecture presented throughout this guide.
